entries.title: Drumstep
entries.tempo_range: 160-180 BPM (programmed so the beat reads as half-time against this faster tempo)

entries.title: Ghetto House
entries.tempo_range: 125-135 BPM (later Dance Mania-era productions pushed toward 140+ BPM as a direct precursor to juke)

entries.title: Halftime
entries.tempo_range: 80-86 BPM (perceived pulse; programmed as the half-time feel of a 160-172 BPM drum and bass tempo)

entries.title: Psybient
entries.tempo_range: 60-110 BPM (often beatless or with only a loose, non-danceable pulse)

entries.title: Ambient
entries.tempo_range: No fixed tempo; when a pulse exists, 50-90 BPM

entries.title: Ambient Dub
entries.tempo_range: No fixed tempo; beatless or loose/rubato, occasionally 60-90 BPM when a skeletal riddim is present

entries.title: Dark Ambient
entries.tempo_range: No fixed tempo; freeform, occasionally 40-70 BPM when a pulse exists

entries.title: Drone
common_keys: or A as common drone fundamentals"

entries.title: Electroclash
entries.subgenre_of: Electro / Synth-pop (crossover with punk/new wave attitude)

entries.title: New Age
entries.tempo_range: No fixed tempo; when a pulse exists, 60-96 BPM

entries.title: Space Ambient
entries.tempo_range: No fixed tempo; when sequenced, 60-100 BPM

entries.title: Vaporwave
entries.tempo_range: 50-100 BPM (frequently a slowed-down version of a source track's original tempo)
common_keys: Determined by sampled source material — commonly C major, F major, A minor
Pitched down from original, often landing in ambiguous/detuned territory

entries.title: Chipmunk Soul
common_keys: shifted up by the varispeed technique"

entries.title: Avant-Garde Jazz
common_keys: Ambiguous/polytonal/atonal, organized by notated compositional plan

entries.title: Atmospheric Black Metal
entries.tempo_range: 70-180 BPM (long-form pieces frequently shift tempo within a single track)

entries.title: Avant-Garde Metal
common_keys: No consistent tonal center by design

entries.title: Blackgaze
entries.tempo_range: 90-200 BPM (blast-beat sections contrasted with slower, dreamier passages)
common_keys: "C major/A minor (relative major shifts for a brighter
more uplifting quality than traditional black metal)"

entries.title: Brutal Death Metal
entries.tempo_range: 100-220 BPM (mid-tempo slam sections alternating with blast-beat passages)
common_keys: "No fixed tonal center; guitar material is organized around low
dissonant chromatic clusters rather than functional key centers"

entries.title: Deathcore
entries.tempo_range: 80-240 BPM (varies drastically between blast-beat and breakdown sections)

entries.title: Drone Metal
common_keys: No fixed tonal center; low sustained root tones

entries.title: Depressive Suicidal Black Metal
entries.tempo_range: 50-160 BPM (frequently very slow, doom-influenced passages alongside faster tremolo/blast sections)

entries.title: Heavy Metal
related_genres: NWOBHM (New Wave of British Heavy Metal)

entries.title: Mathcore
entries.tempo_range: highly variable, frequent abrupt tempo changes within songs

entries.title: New Wave of British Heavy Metal (NWOBHM)
entries.genre_name: New Wave of British Heavy Metal (NWOBHM)

entries.title: Progressive Death Metal
entries.tempo_range: 50-200 BPM (extreme tempo range within a single track is the genre's structural norm, not an exception)

entries.title: Technical Death Metal
common_keys: E minor (frequently treated chromatically/atonally rather than functionally)

entries.title: Pop Rap
related_genres: Hip-Hop (parent tradition, distinct in function and audience)

entries.title: Garage Rock
entries.subgenre_of: Rock and Roll / Rhythm and Blues (raw amateur-band lineage)

entries.title: Jam Band
entries.subgenre_of: Psychedelic Rock / Roots Rock (improvisational live-performance lineage)
entries.tempo_range: 70-160 BPM (highly variable within a single jam)

entries.title: Mod Revival
entries.subgenre_of: Punk Rock / New Wave (1960s mod-revivalist movement)

entries.title: Progressive Rock
entries.tempo_range: 60-160 BPM (highly variable within a single piece)

entries.title: Space Rock
entries.tempo_range: 60-120 BPM (often slow-building, with faster hypnotic driving sections)

entries.title: Stoner Rock
entries.tempo_range: 60-110 BPM (slow, heavy grooves dominate; occasional faster punk-informed sections)

entries.title: Forró
entries.tempo_range: 90-130 BPM across the trio's core rhythms (baião, xote, arrasta-pé)

entries.title: Fuji
common_keys: "Percussion-and-voice driven; modern keyboard-augmented fuji commonly uses A minor

entries.title: Gqom
entries.tempo_range: 120-127 BPM (sgubhu strand often 123-124 BPM; harder gqom tech variants exceed 130 BPM)
common_keys: "Not harmonically centered — gqom is built on rhythm
and repeated vocal/synth stabs rather than functional key/chord structure"

entries.title: Rebetiko
entries.tempo_range: 80-130 BPM depending on dance rhythm (zeibekiko typically slower and freer, hasapiko/hasaposerviko faster and more driving)
common_keys: Not fixed Western major/minor keys — built on Greek dromoi (modal scales) derived from Ottoman/Arabic makam

entries.title: Samba
entries.tempo_range: 90-120 BPM for samba de mesa/pagode, up to 130-145 BPM for fast, driving samba-enredo Carnival parade performances

entries.title: Taarab
entries.tempo_range: 60-100 BPM in classical/orchestral taarab, often with free, rubato vocal phrasing; faster, dance-oriented tempos in modern taarab ya kisasa
common_keys: Not Western-key-centered — organized around Arabic maqamat (modal scale-and-phrase systems) rather than major/minor tonality

entries.title: Vallenato
entries.tempo_range: 80-150 BPM, varying by aire (son slowest, puya fastest)

entries.title: Zouk
entries.tempo_range: 70-100 BPM for zouk love ballads, 120-145 BPM for uptempo zouk béton/carnival zouk
